title: 1 in 50 million: rare ‘split-lobster’ caught off Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News The chances of finding a split-lobster are one in 50 million, but crew members on a fishing boat beat those odds and caught one off of Cape Cod last week, a fishing company said.
The split-lobster in a white container.
Wellfleet Shellfish Company By Carson Lyle April 21, 2026 | 6:51 PM 1 minute to read You have a higher probability of being struck by lightning than finding a split-lobster.

title: 26-year-old identified as man killed by police in Lexington
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Local News Police responded to a home in Lexington on Saturday after a resident reported that their son had injured himself with a knife, the DA said previously.
Prosecutors identified a man shot and killed by police in Lexington over the weekend after he allegedly approached officers with a knife.
Colin Barthelemy, 26, of Lexington was shot by police on Saturday, the office of Middlesex District Attorney Marian Ryan said in a press release Tuesday evening.

title: ​Al Horford offers deeper explanation on cryptic comments about Celtics departure
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics "I knew it was time for me to move on.
" Al Horford has not decided yet about returning for a 20th NBA season.

AP Photo/Kelley L Cox By Conor Ryan April 21, 2026 | 6:08 PM 3 minutes to read Al Horford raised some eyebrows earlier this year when the former Celtics big man offered up a few ambiguous comments about his decision to leave Boston in free agency last summer.

title: Amtrak releases first look at new long distance fleet
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: New England Travel The new rail cars will "bring travelers the world-class service they deserve.
" Amtrak Amtrak A new era in American train travel is coming, Amtrak says.
The company announced on Wednesday that it has issued a formal request for suppliers to bid on its new long-distance fleet replacement contract.

title: Clarke’s, a 50-year-old bar at Faneuil Hall, has closed
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Food News The owners told The Boston Globe that foot traffic downtown had not bounced back since the pandemic.
Clarke's has closed after 50 years in business.
Jonathan Wiggs/Globe Staff ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: With the Boston Marathon finish line in sight, they sacrificed personal goals to help a runner in need
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Boston Marathon Israel Rivera and Henco Visser embodied the spirit of the 2026 Boston Marathon when they helped Jonathan Adams cross the finish line on Monday.
Israel Rivera (L) and Henco Visser (R) carry Jonathan Adams along Boylston Street at the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Autumn Sloboda / Boston.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor indicted on 81 new sexual assault charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Dr.
Derrick Todd is accused of performing unnecessary breast and pelvic exams on patients.
He now faces more than 100 sexual assault charges in all.
